prompt:
The supplier feed ingester is quietly mangling data and it only shows up under the new transfer layer, which hands us the stream in tiny byte chunks instead of one big read. Two symptoms from production:

1) Any record with a line break inside a quoted field comes out glued together — a note that reads 'two\r\nline note' in the source file lands in the DB as 'twoline note'. No error, no problem report, the field is just silently wrong. Record counts look fine so nobody noticed for a week.

2) When the feed starts with a UTF-8 BOM AND arrives in small chunks, the first header column comes back as '\ufeffsku' instead of 'sku', so every row keys wrong. Feed the exact same bytes in one big chunk and it's fine. To be clear about the contract: the BOM is consumed only at the very start of the stream — a U+FEFF later in the data is legitimate field content and must survive.

Also, while diffing against the source files we found a truncated feed (connection died mid-record inside an open quote) produced no problem entry at all — the half record just evaporated. Malformed rows are supposed to be reported with the physical line number where the record starts.

`python3 test_feedcsv.py` replays all of this — same fixture at chunk sizes 1 through 8 and whole-blob must give byte-identical reports (rows, exact start line numbers, problems). Fix feedcsv.py. Tests stay untouched.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: build_cmd exits 0 on the buggy fixture; test_feedcsv.py exits 1 with AssertionError in test_blank_line_inside_quoted_field: a quoted field spanning physical lines comes back with its embedded newlines silently dropped ('topbottom' instead of 'top\n\nbottom'); the sample-feed checks also fail — at chunk sizes 1-2 the header decodes as '\ufeffsku', and the truncated-feed check fails because the unterminated trailing record vanishes without a problem entry
